conbody.p: Overview: The Ranked Encounters dashboard helps CDI managers to gain insights into ranked encounters that CDSs have reviewed, optimize resource allocation, and enhance the overall productivity of the team.

By default, the report displays data for last 9 calendar months based on the admission date. The dashboard also helps you group data by week, month, quarter, and year and analyze trends over different time periods and analyze them more easily.
Navigation: On the Analytics home page left side panel, navigate to CDE Triage Triage and CDS Assistant Usage Ranked Encounters .

The Ranked Encounters dashboard displays the following:
KPI Cards: Total Encounters, Encounters Reviewed, Encounters Ranked, and Ranked Encounters Reviewed
Encounters Reviewed
Encounters Reviewed Summary

li.p: Encounters that have been given a rank in CDE Triage are called ranked encounters. The current rank of an encounter may be blank.
Encounters are ranked based on the rules that are defined for the prioritization within CDE Triage.

ul.li:
The following encounter types are considered:
Both ranked (standard and custom) and unranked encounters
Highest rank of the encounter
Count of all encounters that have been assigned a rank and have an initial review date/time.
All encounter statuses except 'Review Not Needed' are considered.

conbody.p: On the top panel, you can select desired values for the slicers (refer ) and the corresponding data gets filtered in the charts.
For filters on all pages, refer .

row.entry:
Displays total encounters for the selected "View By" option.
Total Encounters: Count of inpatient admissions or discharges with status other than 'Review Not Needed'.

entry.p: Displays count and percentage of encounters reviewed with or without clarifications.
% Encounters Reviewed = Encounters Reviewed / Total Encounters x 100. This value is rounded to 0 decimal places. For example, 80%.
Displays the count and percentage of encounters ranked for the selected time period.
Encounters Ranked: The count of encounters that have ever been assigned a standard or custom rank in CDE Triage. The current rank for the encounter may be blank.
% Encounters Ranked = Encounters Ranked / Total Encounters x 100. This value is rounded to 0 decimal places, for example, 30%.
Total Encounters: Count of inpatient admissions or discharges with status other than 'Review Not Needed'.
Displays the count and percentage of ranked encounters reviewed for the selected time period. This count includes all encounters that have been assigned a rank and have an initial review date/time
Format: Ranked Encounters Reviewed (% Ranked Encounters Reviewed) 67 (89%).
Ranked Encounters Reviewed: The count of ranked encounters reviewed with or without clarifications.
% Ranked Encounters Reviewed = Ranked Encounters Reviewed / Encounters Ranked x 100. This value is rounded to 0 decimal places, for example, 30%.

conbody.p: This chart allows users to analyze volume and percentage of reviews for each rank category for the selected 'View By' option.

Info Icon(Text on hover): Includes encounters other than 'Review Not Needed'. The visual shows the data for all standard and custom ranks by default. For data on encounters with specific ranks, use the 'Highest Rank’ slicer.

row.entry: Displays the count of reviewed encounters for each standard or custom rank. Encounters that were reviewed but do not have a rank are listed under 'Unranked' category.Ranked Encounters that were reviewed (have initial review date/time) but were later changed to "Review Not Needed" are excluded from this metric calculations.

conbody.p: This chart helps users analyze how many reviews each rank category has for every CDS during the chosen time period. It displays the breakdown of encounters reviewed by the rank category for the selected 'View By' option.

Info Icon(Text on hover): Includes encounters other than 'Review Not Needed'. The visual shows the data for all standard and custom ranks by default. For data on encounters with specific ranks, use the 'Highest Rank’ slicer.

row.entry: Displays the count of reviewed encounters for each standard or custom rank category. Reviewed encounters that do not have a rank assigned are grouped under the 'Unranked' category.When you choose the 'View By' option as CDS, you are looking at the CDS that conducted the initial review. This count includes all ranked encounters that had an initial review date/time. However, encounters that were initially reviewed but later marked as "Review Not Needed" are excluded from this calculation.

When this option is selected, all charts in the dashboard display the data by month of admission in ascending order. The dashboard also helps you group data by week, month, quarter, and year and analyze trends over different time periods and analyze them more easily.
When the ‘View By’ is Admit Month, the data in all the charts is sorted by Admit month in ascending order.Admit Month is selected as the default 'View By' option.

entry.p: When this option is selected, all the charts in the dashboard display the data by month of discharge in ascending order. The dashboard also helps you group data by week, month, quarter, and year and analyze trends over different time periods and analyze them more easily.
When the ‘View By’ is Discharge Month, the data in all the charts is sorted by Discharge month in ascending order.
When this option is selected, all the charts in the dashboard display the data by facility in ascending order.
When the ‘View By’ is Facility, the data in all the charts is sorted by Facility name in ascending order.
When this option is selected, all the charts in the dashboard display the data by payer in ascending order.
When the ‘View By’ is Payer, the data in all the charts is sorted by Payer in ascending order.

row.entry:
When this option is selected, all the charts in the dashboard display the data by CDS.
When the ‘View By’ is CDS, the data in all the charts is sorted by CDS name in ascending order.

li.p: Allows user to select the admit date range.
Format: Between date range, example: 1/1/2023 - 9/30/2023.

ul.li: Default: Last 9 calendar months date range selected by default.
Displays the discharge date range.
With the Reset button, you can reset the discharge date range.
Tooltip: Ctrl + Click to reset both Admit Date and Discharge Date.
Displays both active and inactive facilities in alphabetical order that you have access to.
You can select multiple facilities.
You can search the facility by Facility ID or Description.
Format: Facility Description (Facility ID)
Default: ALL
Displays both active and inactive payers in alphabetical order associated with the facilities you have access to.
You can select multiple payers.
"Unassigned Payer" displays data for the encounters that do not have a payer assigned.
You can search the Payer by Payer ID or Description.
Format: Payer Description (Payer ID)
Default: ALL

li.p: Displays both active and inactive CDSs in alphabetical order associated with the facilities user has access to.
User can select multiple CDSs
"Unassigned CDS" displays data for the encounters that do not have a CDS assigned.
Users can search the CDS by CDS ID or Name.
Format: CDS Name (CDS ID)
Default: ALL

ul.li: This filter displays the list of available units.
You can select multiple Units.
You can search the Unit.
Default: ALL

li.p: Displays all standard and custom ranks that can be assigned to the encounters.
User can select multiple ranks.
Users can search the rank number.
“Unranked” displays data for all encounters that are not ranked.
Format: Rank # ( such as Rank 1, Rank 2,)
Default: ALL

entry.note: This slicer filters the data based on the highest standard/custom rank ever assigned to an encounter (where 1 is the highest standard rank and 11 is the lowest standard rank).

row.entry:
These options are available only when the View By option is selected as Admit Date/Discharge Date.
See .

ul.li: Displays the available patient types. Example: Inpatient, Outpatient.
Default: Inpatient
Displays the list of available admit service.
You can select multiple Units.
You can search the Admit Service.
Default: ALL
Displays the available patient types.Example: ED, IP, etc.
You can select multiple Visit Type.
You can search the Visit Type.
Default: ALL

li.p: Displays the available options for Weekend Only Stay- Y and N.
User can select more than one option.
Weekend Only Stay = Y (Unassigned encounters admitted after Friday, 6PM and Discharged before Sunday, 11.59 PM).
Default : ALL

row.entry: Count of inpatient admissions or discharges with a status other than 'Review Not Needed'.
The count of encounters reviewed with or without clarifications. % Encounters Reviewed = Encounters Reviewed / Total Encounters x 100. (Rounded to 0 decimal places) E.g., 80%.

The count of encounters that have ever been assigned a standard or custom rank in CDE Triage. The current rank for the encounter may be blank; % Encounters Ranked = Encounters Ranked / Total Encounters x 100. (Rounded to 0 decimal places) E.g., 30%. Total Encounters: Count of inpatient admissions or discharges with status other than 'Review Not Needed'.

The count of encounters that have never been assigned a standard or custom rank in CDE Triage. % Unranked Encounters = Unranked Encounters / Total Encounters x 100. (Rounded to 0 decimal places) E.g., 30%. Total Encounters: Count of inpatient admissions or discharges with status other than 'Review Not Needed'.
The count of ranked encounters reviewed with or without clarifications. % Ranked Encounters Reviewed = Ranked Encounters Reviewed / Encounters Ranked x 100. (Rounded to 0 decimal places) E.g., 30%.
